text.title: React Hooks Audit Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure: install rules, scan, classify, fix, lock.

step.name: install-lint
step.action: Install `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` (peer-dep on ESLint 8/9). Add `templates/eslint-react-hooks.config.js` block to `eslint.config.js`. Set both `rules-of-hooks` and `exhaustive-deps` to `error`.

step.name: scan
step.action: Run `eslint src --format json` and pipe to `scripts/parse-react-hooks-audit.js` (or jq) to produce a violations array conforming to the audit schema in 02-output-contract.xml.

step.name: classify
step.action: For each violation: classify as one of {conditional-hook-call, stale-closure, sync-via-effect, cleanup-missing, memo-bloat}. Match against 03-failure-modes.xml.
step.decision-gate: If >100 violations: fix in priority order — high severity first; do not boil-the-ocean in one PR.

step.name: fix
step.action: For each violation apply the fix from 03-failure-modes.xml. Run unit tests after each component fix. For sync-via-effect: convert to direct derivation. For cleanup-missing: add return value. For exhaustive-deps: add deps OR wrap in `useRef`.

step.name: lock
step.action: Add the ESLint config to pre-commit hook. Add an action in CI workflow to run `eslint --max-warnings 0`. Any future PR that re-introduces a violation fails the gate.
step.output: gate locked; new violations cannot land
